question: In the programming language CortexScript, what does the '->>' operator do?

answers.original:
The `->>` operator in CortexScript is used to channel the output of one asynchronous Cognate function directly into another.

### References

- [1] ->> Operator

entities.name: ->> Operator
entities.description: The ->> operator in CortexScript is used to channel the output of one asynchronous Cognate function directly into another.

entities.name: => Operator
entities.description: The => operator in CortexScript is used for defining anonymous lambda functions.

entities.name: := Operator

relations.description: The ->> operator in CortexScript is used to pipe the results from one asynchronous Cognate into the next one in a pipeline.
The => operator in CortexScript is used for defining anonymous lambda functions.
The := operator is the standard assignment operator in CortexScript.
